## Acceptance Criteria

**FAIL**

- `jit validate <id> --explain` does not show whether a state predicate matched. `explain_rules` only includes rules from `ruleset.matching_rules(&issue)` at [crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:572](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:572), so any rule excluded by `when.state` is omitted entirely. The CLI then prints only the already-matched selector string at [crates/jit/src/main.rs:3643](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:3643). This cannot explain “state predicate did not match,” which is an explicit success criterion.
- Existing tests reinforce that non-matching rules disappear from explain output: [crates/jit/tests/validate_rule_runner_tests.rs:187](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/validate_rule_runner_tests.rs:187).

Other criteria appear satisfied: `when.state` supports single/list values, combines with other selectors, validates invalid names at load, and docs were updated.
